label: Localized nail polish contact rash without infection or anaphylaxis
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized nail polish or nail product contact skin reaction.

No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, cellulitis, abscess, felon/tenosynovitis concern, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, cellulitis, abscess, felon/tenosynovitis concern, rapidly progressive rash, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy/wound plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, hand clinic, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized nail polish contact skin reaction with anaphylaxis, infection, abscess/felon, mucosal, pregnancy/pediatric, severe-rash, and specialist-directed pathways absent.
